meta.title: Guided tours
meta.intro: Expert-Hour tours: guided paths through the doors of this site for the three visitors who most often turn up. Pick the one that fits you. Each tour is a short ordered walk — ten stops — that answers the question that visitor actually asks, in the order they'd ask it. These are the same paths the daily HARDEN walk uses to keep every door honest.

personas.label: The Regulator
personas.one_liner: Is this business inside the rules, and can it prove it when it isn't?
personas.intro: You care about compliance, consumer protection and whether claims survive scrutiny. This walk goes straight at the obligations, the honesty surfaces, and the places the company is allowed to be wrong.

stops.title: Start at the honest gap
stops.why: The company cannot see inside its own market. Everything downstream is an approximation — see the gap stated openly before you judge any number.

stops.title: The obligations register
stops.look_at: Regulatory obligations keyed by regime and date
stops.why: Each rule carries an effective-from / effective-to date, so a rule only ever binds over the window it was actually in force.

stops.title: The price cap in force
stops.why: The binding domestic pricing constraint, indexed to the quarter it took effect — the first thing a regulator checks.

stops.title: Held and rejected
stops.look_at: Controls that stopped a bad artefact shipping
stops.why: A control is only evidence if it can fire. See the holds it actually raised, not a green light with nothing behind it.

stops.title: Mutation-tested controls
stops.why: Each control here has been deliberately broken to prove it fires (R15). A control that cannot fail is theatre — this is where the theatre is caught.

stops.title: Open defects, in public
stops.why: Honesty is a visitor surface here, not a buried artefact. Known problems are shown, not hidden.

stops.title: Incident to rule
stops.why: Every hard rule (R1–R15) traces to a real mistake. See how a failure became a standing control.

stops.title: Every number's clock
stops.why: No financial figure is published without its clock. A basis-less number is treated as a defect (R14).

stops.title: What's provisional
stops.why: Where the model is deliberately simpler than reality, it says so — with the simplification named and registered.

stops.title: The predictions ledger
stops.why: The strongest evidence a claim is honest: a dated forecast sitting next to what actually happened. Close the tour here.

personas.label: The Investor
personas.one_liner: Does it make money, does the money survive a crisis, and can I trust the figures?
personas.intro: You care about margin, cash, risk and the credibility of the numbers. This walk goes at the P&L, the treasury, the crisis the business priced through, and the proof that the headline figures are not marketing.

stops.title: The thesis
stops.look_at: Front-door pulse strip and thesis chart
stops.why: The claim in one line, with the live book, treasury and enterprise value — and a link to the last real bill behind it.

stops.title: Net position
stops.why: The bottom line, with its basis stated. Start from the number everyone quotes, then go behind it.

stops.title: The margin bridge
stops.why: Margin decomposed into its drivers — treated as a diagnostic, never a target to tune toward (R12).

stops.title: Segment economics
stops.why: Flat pricing makes some customers net-negative. See which segments actually pay their way.

stops.title: Treasury vs profit
stops.why: Accounting profit is not cash. See what actually lands after settlement lag and hedging margin calls.

stops.title: The crisis it survived
stops.look_at: The 2021–22 price spike in the realised path
stops.why: Around thirty real UK suppliers failed here. The company priced through the same realised prices, blindfolded.

stops.title: Risk position
stops.look_at: Value at Risk and hedge fraction
stops.why: How exposed the book is, and how much is bought forward — built from what the company could observe, not from ground truth.

stops.title: Enterprise value, labelled
stops.why: The valuation is an estimate and says so. Provisional figures here are explicitly retractable.

stops.title: The verification stack
stops.look_at: How each figure is checked to the rendered pixel
stops.why: Every user-visible number is verified against the live surface (R11), not just the code that made it.

stops.title: Predictions vs realised
stops.why: The centrepiece: dated forecasts against outcomes. If the figures were marketing, this is where it would show. Close here.

personas.label: The Engineer
personas.one_liner: How is this actually built, and where is the hard part?
personas.intro: You care about the architecture, the seams, the data and how correctness is enforced. This walk goes at the wall, the simulation depth, the platform, and the method that keeps it all honest.

stops.title: The wall as an interface
stops.why: The core architectural constraint: the company layer reads observables only, never simulation internals. The seam is the go-live seam.

stops.title: Simulation depth
stops.look_at: The realised world the company is blindfolded to
stops.why: Half-hourly settlement against the real Elexon record since 2016 — the ground truth the company has to approximate.

stops.title: The data catalogue
stops.why: Where the ground truth comes from. Real data in, no synthetic price path for the settled history.

stops.title: The platform
stops.look_at: The software that runs the company
stops.why: The billing, settlement and decision machinery — the parts that would swap for real endpoints behind unchanged interfaces at go-live.

stops.title: The coupled triad
stops.look_at: SIM depth ↔ company coping ↔ measured gap
stops.why: Every capability is a three-loop, and the belief-vs-truth gap is the score. This is where the gap per pair is reported.

stops.title: The maturity map
stops.why: The build backlog as a map. Levels are claims about how thoroughly something is built and verified — and can be demoted.

stops.title: The epoch arc
stops.why: The narrative order of the build vs the compounding-return order it's actually built in. Exit tests are the only gate.

stops.title: Controls that can fail
stops.why: The engineering heart of the honesty machinery: a control counts only once a mutation test proves it fires on its own defect (R15).

stops.title: Incident to rule
stops.why: The rule set is not designed up front — each rule is forged from a specific real failure. Read how.

stops.title: The method, whole
stops.look_at: How the pieces fit into a repeatable way of building
stops.why: Step back and see the method as one system: wall, triad, maturity map, mutation-tested controls. Close here.
